options.content:
- This dashboard mimics [VictoriaMetrics Cardinality Explorer in vmui](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/#cardinality-explorer). In case of problems, it is better to use `vmui` directly.
- Dashboard requires [Infinity datasource](https://grafana.com/grafana/plugins/yesoreyeram-infinity-datasource/) to be installed and configured to have access to VM: `http://victoriametrics:8428` for vmsingle, `http://vmauth:8427/select/<tenant>/prometheus` for cluster.
- Dashboard can display data only for a specific `day`. For example, if you want to view data for the 3rd day of the month, select the period from the 2nd to the 3rd day.
- In tables, it automatically compares cardinality numbers with numbers 1d before. It should help to track changes in the number of series with time.

- Identify and eliminate labels with frequently changed values to reduce their [cardinality](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-high-cardinality) and [high churn rate](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/faq/#what-is-high-churn-rate)
- Find unused time series and [drop entire metrics](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/relabeling/)
- Aggregate time series before they got ingested into the database via [streaming aggregation](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/stream-aggregation/)

panels.title: Metrics with a high number of series

options.content:
- Decrease the number of unique label values to reduce cardinality
- Drop the label entirely via [relabeling](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/relabeling/)
- For volatile label values (such as URL path, user session, etc.) consider printing them to the log file instead of adding to time series

panels.title: Labels with a high number of unique values

options.content:
* `Limit entries` - limits the amount of entries each table could show.
* `Time series selector` - accepts arbitrary time series selector for filtering metrics. For example, `__name__!~"go_.*"` will display only metrics with prefix `go_`. Gets automatically updated when clicking on metric name in `Metric names` table, or label name in labels tables.
* `Focus label` - is used for displaying details for a specific label. Gets automatically updated when clicking on label name in labels tables.

Press on `Reset Filters` to return all filters to default values

1. `Request count` and `Last request` columns help to identify how many times and when it was the last time metric was queried. See more about [metric usage tracking](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/single-server-victoriametrics/#track-ingested-metrics-usage). If metric wasn't used for a long time (e.g. >20d), it might not worth to store it anymore. 
2. `1d` and `1d[%]` columns display the change in cardinality numbers between selected day and day before. Could help identifying rapidly growing metrics.
3. Click on the metric name to see details about its labels in tables below.
4. Click on the label name to see its values in the table in the bottom-right table.

panels.description:
Based on [metric usage tracker](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victoriametrics/#track-ingested-metrics-usage).

Columns `Requests count` and `Last request` depend on this panel.

* `Tracking since` shows for what period of time metrics usage tracking is On.
* `Metric names` - shows the total number of unique metric names in the system
panels.title: Total metric names
Metric names with the highest number of series

panels.description:
Shows the total number of unique time series in selected and previous to it day.

Used as source of data for other panels.

targets.root_selector:
.data as $root |
[
  ($root.seriesCountByMetricName       | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, lastRequest: (.lastRequestTimestamp * 1000), shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByMetricName"     })),
  ($root.seriesCountByLabelName        | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByLabelName"      })),
  ($root.seriesCountByFocusLabelValue  | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByFocusLabelValue"})),
  ($root.seriesCountByLabelValuePair   | map((.name | split("=")) as $splitName | . + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByLabelValuePair", labelName: $splitName[0], labelValue: $splitName[1] })),
  ($root.labelValueCountByLabelName    | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "labelValueCountByLabelName", }))
]
| add

.data as $root |
[
  ($root.seriesCountByMetricName       | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, lastRequest: (.lastRequestTimestamp * 1000), shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByMetricName"     })),
  ($root.seriesCountByLabelName        | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByLabelName"      })),
  ($root.seriesCountByFocusLabelValue  | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByFocusLabelValue"})),
  ($root.seriesCountByLabelValuePair   | map((.name | split("=")) as $splitName | . + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "seriesCountByLabelValuePair", labelName: $splitName[0], labelValue: $splitName[1] })),
  ($root.labelValueCountByLabelName    | map(. + {total: $root.totalSeries, shareOfTotal: (.value / $root.totalSeries), type: "labelValueCountByLabelName", }))
]
| add

panels.title: Total time series
Labels with the highest number of series
Values for "$focus" label with the highest number of series
Label=value pairs with the highest number of series
Labels with the highest number of unique values
